17	## Introduction
18	
19	This repository provides opinionated infrastructure-as-code examples to automate the creation of virtual machine images and their guest operating systems on Proxmox using [HashiCorp Packer][packer] and the [Packer Plugin for Proxmox][packer-plugin-proxmox] (`proxmox-iso` builder). All examples are authored in the HashiCorp Configuration Language ("HCL2").
20	
21	By default, the machine image artifacts are converted to templates within Proxmox after a virtual machine is built and configured according to the individual templates.
22	
23	The following builds are available:
24

48	## Requirements
49	
50	**Operating Systems**:
51	
52	Operating systems and versions tested with the project:
53	
54	- Proxmox PVE Version >= 8
55	- Ubuntu Server 22.04 LTS (`x86_64`)
56	- CentOS Stream 9 (`x86_64`)
57	
58	**Packer**:
59	
60	- HashiCorp [Packer][packer-install] 1.12.0 or higher.
61	
62	  > **Note**
63	  >
64	  > Click on the operating system name to display the installation steps.
65	
66	  - <details>
67	      <summary>Ubuntu</summary>
68	
69	    The Terraform packages are signed using a private key controlled by HashiCorp, so you must configure your system to trust that HashiCorp key for package authentication.
70	
71	    To configure your repository:
72	
73	    ```shell
74	    sudo bash -c 'wget -O- https://apt.releases.hashicorp.com/gpg | gpg --dearmor > /usr/share/keyrings/hashicorp-archive-keyring.gpg'
75	    ```
76	
77	    Verify the key's fingerprint:
78	
79	    ```shell
80	    gpg --no-default-keyring --keyring /usr/share/keyrings/hashicorp-archive-keyring.gpg --fingerprint
81	    ```
82	
83	    The fingerprint must match E8A0 32E0 94D8 EB4E A189 D270 DA41 8C88 A321 9F7B. You can also verify the key on [Security at HashiCorp][hcp-security] under Linux Package Checksum Verification.
84	
85	    Add the official HashiCorp repository to your system:
86	
87	    ```shell
88	    sudo bash -c 'echo "deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/hashicorp-archive-keyring.gpg] \
89	    https://apt.releases.hashicorp.com $(lsb_release -cs) main" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/hashicorp.list'
90	    ```
91	
92	    Install Packer from HashiCorp repository:
93	
94	    ```shell
95	    sudo apt update && sudo apt install packer
96	    ```
97	
98	    </details>
99	
100	  - <details>
101	      <summary>CentOS Stream 9</summary>
102	
103	    Install `yum-config-manager` to manage your repositories.
104	
105	    ```shell
106	    sudo yum install -y yum-utils
107	    ```
108	
109	    Use `yum-config-manager` to add the official HashiCorp Linux repository:
110	
111	    ```shell
112	    sudo yum-config-manager --add-repo https://rpm.releases.hashicorp.com/RHEL/hashicorp.repo
113	    ```
114	
115	    Install.
116	
117	    ```shell
118	    sudo yum -y install packer
119	    ```
120	    </details>
121	
122	
123	- Packer plugins:
124	
125	  > **Note**
126	  >
127	  > Required plugins are automatically downloaded and initialized when using `./build.sh`. For dark sites, you may download the plugins and place these same directory as your Packer executable `/usr/local/bin` or `$HOME/.packer.d/plugins`.
128	
129	  - [Packer Plugin for Git][packer-plugin-git] 0.6.2 or later - a community plugin for HashiCorp Packer.
130	  - HashiCorp [Packer Plugin for Proxmox-ISO][packer-plugin-proxmox] version 1.2.1 - the plugin for HashiCorp Packer to communicate with Proxmox VE. This needs to be pinned to version 1.2.1 at this time due to a [CPU bug](https://github.com/hashicorp/packer-plugin-proxmox/issues/307).
131	
132	**Ansible**:
133	
134	- [Ansible][ansible] [Core][ansible-core] version 2.14 or higher.
135	
136	  > **Note**
137	  >
138	  > Click on the operating system name to display the installation steps.
139	
140	  - <details>
141	      <summary>Ubuntu</summary>
142	
143	    It is recommended that you install ansible-core using your system's package manager instead of via pip.
144	
145	    Refresh the repositories:
146	    ```shell
147	    sudo apt update
148	    ```
149	
150	    Install software-properties-common:
151	    ```shell
152	    sudo apt install -y software-properties-common
153	    ```
154	
155	    Add the Ansible repository to your system:
156	
157	    ```shell
158	    sudo add-apt-repository --yes --update ppa:ansible/ansible
159	    ```
160	
161	    Install ansible-core from the Ansible repository:
162	
163	    ```shell
164	    sudo apt install -y ansible-core
165	    ```
166	
167	    </details>
168	
169	
170	  - <details>
171	      <summary>CentOS Stream 9</summary>
172	
173	    It is recommended that you install ansible using your system's package manager instead of via pip.
174	
175	    Install.
176	
177	    ```shell
178	    dnf -y install ansible
179	    ```
180	    </details>
181	
182	
183	**Platform**:
184	
185	- Proxmox PVE 8.0 or later.
186	
187	# Download
188	
189	After installing the required software, the quickest way to get building is to clone this repository.
190	
191	```shell
192	git clone https://github.com/traefikturkey/oncall.git
193	cd oncall
194	```
195	
196	The following table describes the directory structure.
197	
198	| Directory       | Description                                                                              |
199	| :---            | :---                                                                                     |
200	| **`ansible`**   | Contains the Ansible roles to prepare Linux machine image builds.                        |
201	| **`builds`**    | Contains the templates, variables, and configuration files for the machine image builds. |
202	| **`manifests`** | Manifests created after the completion of the machine image builds.                      |
203	
204	
205	# Configuration
206	
207	## Example Variables
208	
209	The project includes example variables files that you can use as a starting point for your own configuration.
210	
211	The [variables][packer-variables] are defined in `.pkrvars.hcl` files.
212	
213	Run the config script `./config.sh` to copy the `.pkrvars.hcl.example` files to a `config` directory.
214	
215	```shell
216	./config.sh
217	./build.sh
218	```
219	
220	The `config` folder is the default folder. You can override the default by passing an alternate value as the first argument.
221	
222	For example:
223	
224	San Francisco: `us-west-1`
225	
226	```shell
227	./config.sh us-west-1
228	./build.sh us-west-1
229	```
230	
231	Los Angeles: `us-west-2`
232	
233	```shell
234	./config.sh us-west-2
235	./build.sh us-west-2
236	```
237	
238	This is useful for the purposes of running machine image builds for different environment.
239	
240	## Configuration Variables
241	
242	## Proxmox Virtual Machine IDs
243	Each template has a preset static vm_id.
244	
245	These can be changed by editing the `./config/*/(linux or windows)-(*).pkr.hcl` files.
246	
247	### Ansible Variables
248	
249	Edit the `config/ansible.pkrvars.hcl` file to configure the credentials for the Ansible account on Linux machine images.
250	
251	```hcl title="config/ansible.pkrvars.hcl"
252	ansible_username = "ansible"
253	ansible_key      = "<public_key>"
254	```
255	
256	**Ansible User Password**
257	
258	A random password is auto-generated for the Ansible user.
259	
260	### Build Variables
261	
262	Edit the `config/build.pkrvars.hcl` file to configure the credentials for the default account on machine images.
263	
264	```hcl title="config/build.pkrvars.hcl"
265	build_username           = "example"
266	build_password           = "<plaintext_password>"
267	build_password_encrypted = "<sha512_encrypted_password>"
268	build_key                = "<public_key>"
269	```
270	
271	You will need to generate a SHA-512 encrypted password for the `build_password_encrypted` using tools like `mkpasswd`.
272	
273	Run the following command to generate a SHA-512 encrypted password:
274	
275	```shell
276	mkpasswd -m sha512crypt
277	```
278	
279	The following output is displayed:
280	
281	```shell
282	Password: ***************
283	[password hash]
284	```
285	
286	Generate a public key for the `build_key` for public key authentication.
287	
288	Run the following command to generate a public key for the `build_key` for public key authentication.
289	
290	```shell
291	ssh-keygen -t ecdsa -b 512 -C "<name@example.com>"
292	```
293	
294	The following output is displayed:
295	
296	```shell
297	Generating public/private ecdsa key pair.
298	Enter file in which to save the key (/Users/example/.ssh/id_ecdsa):
299	Enter passphrase (empty for no passphrase): **************
300	Enter same passphrase again: **************
301	Your identification has been saved in /Users/example/.ssh/id_ecdsa.
302	Your public key has been saved in /Users/example/.ssh/id_ecdsa.pub.
303	```
304	
305	The content of the public key, `build_key`, is added the key to the `~/.ssh/authorized_keys` file of the `build_username` on the Linux guest operating systems.
306	
307	> [!IMPORTANT]
308	>
309	> Make sure to replace the example public keys and passwords!
310	>
311	> By default, both Public Key Authentication and Password Authentication are enabled for Linux distributions.
312	>
313	> If you wish to disable Password Authentication and only use Public Key Authentication, comment or remove the portion of the associated Ansible `configure` role.
314	
315	### Common Variables
316	
317	Edit the `config/common.pkrvars.hcl` file to configure the following common variables:
318	
319	- Removable Media Settings
320	- Boot and Provisioning Settings
321	- HCP Packer Registry
322	
323	```hcl title="config/common.pkrvars.hcl"
324	// Removable Media Settings
325	common_iso_storage = "<Proxmox Storage Location>"
326	
327	// Boot and Provisioning Settings
328	common_data_source       = "http"
329	common_http_interface    = null
330	common_http_bind_address = null
331	common_http_port_min     = 8000
332	common_http_port_max     = 8099
333	common_ip_wait_timeout   = "20m"
334	common_shutdown_timeout  = "15m"
335	
336	// HCP Packer
337	common_hcp_packer_registry_enabled = false
338	```
339	
340	#### Data Source
341	
342	The default provisioning data source for Linux machine image builds is `http`. This is used to serve the kickstart files to the Linux guest operating system during the build.
343	
344	```hcl title="config/common.pkrvars.hcl"
345	common_data_source = "http"
346	```
347	
348	> **Note**
349	>
350	>    Packer includes a built-in HTTP server that is used to serve the kickstart files for Linux machine image builds.
351	>
352	>    If iptables/nftables is enabled on your Packer host, you will need to open `common_http_port_min` through `common_http_port_max` ports.
353	>
354	>    iptables command:
355	>    ```shell
356	>    iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --match multiport --dports 8000:9000 -j ACCEPT
357	>    ```
358	>
359	>    firewall-cmd example:
360	>    ```shell
361	>    firewall-cmd --zone=public --add-port=8000-9000/tcp --permanent
362	>    firewall-cmd --reload
363	>    ```
364	
365	You can change the `common_data_source` from `http` to `disk` to build supported Linux machine images without the need to use Packer's HTTP server. This is useful for environments that may not be able to route back to the system from which Packer is running. For example, building a machine image in VMware Cloud on AWS.
366	
367	```hcl title="config/common.pkrvars.hcl"
368	common_data_source = "disk"
369	```
370	
371	The Packer plugin's `cd_content` option is used when selecting `disk` unless the distribution does not support a secondary CD-ROM.
372	
373	#### HTTP Interface
374	
375	Name of the network interface that Packer gets `HTTPIP` from. Defaults to the first non loopback interface.
376	
377	```hcl title="config/common.pkrvars.hcl"
378	common_http_interface = "eth2"
379	```
380	
381	#### HTTP Bind Address
382	
383	IP address on the build server to bind the Packer HTTP instance to. Must be an interface that is reachable from the Proxmox server.
384	```hcl title="config/common.pkrvars.hcl"
385	common_http_bind_address = 172.16.15.97"
386	```
387	
388	### Network Variables
389	
390	Edit the `config/network.pkrvars.hcl` file to configure the following:
391	
392	#### Proxmox Specific Network Variables for VM Templates
393	These variables are used by Packer to configure the network interface for the VM template. These are specific to your environment. For example, to use the default `vmbr0` interface and the tag for VLAN 102, you would set it as follows:
394	
395	```hcl title="config/network.pkrvars.hcl"
396	// Proxmox settings for VM templates
397	vm_bridge_interface  = "vmbr0"
398	vm_vlan_tag          = "102"
399	```
400	
401	Configuring a static IP address under the `configs/network.pkrvars.hcl` file is supported. If you want to use DHCP for the templates then leave these variables commented out. The default is DHCP.
402	
403	> **Note**
404	>
405	> - These settings are site specific for each Proxmox host and are going to be needed regardless if you use DHCP or static IP addresses.
406	
407	#### Static IP address settings
408	The Packer build templates default to using DHCP, however, you can use static IP addressing for your VM templates. Simply uncomment the following vars and configure to your specific requirements:
409	
410	```hcl title="config/network.pkrvars.hcl"
411	vm_ip_address = "192.168.101.100"
412	vm_ip_netmask = 24
413	vm_ip_gateway = "192.168.101.1"
414	vm_dns_list   = [ "8.8.8.8", "8.8.4.4" ]
415	```
416	
417	> **Note**
418	>
419	> - If you need/want to go back to using DHCP, just comment these variables out again and the templates should go back to using DHCP.
420	
421	### Proxmox Variables
422	
423	Edit the `config/proxmox.pkrvars.hcl` file to configure the following:
424	
425	- Promxox Endpoint and Credentials
426	
427	```hcl title="config/proxmox.pkrvars.hcl"
428	// Proxmox Credentials
429	proxmox_api_token_id        = "name@realm!token"
430	proxmox_api_token_secret    = "<token secret>"
431	proxmox_insecure_connection = false
432	
433	// Proxmox Specific Settings
434	proxmox_hostname = "<FQDN or IP of proxmox server>"
435	proxmox_node     = "<proxmox node name>"
436	```
437	
438	The `proxmox_api_token_id` variable uses a specific format and, as the time of this writing, needs to be assigned to the `PVEAdmin` role. One of the to-do's is to document a least-privilege method of creating the Proxmox API token.
439	
440	For more information, please see the [Proxmox documentation][proxmox-api-tokens] on authentication.
441	
442	For Proxmox installs that use a self-signed certificate, you will want to set `proxmox_insecure_connection` to `true`.
443	
444	### Storage Variables
445	
446	Edit the `config/linux-storage.pkrvars.hcl` file to configure storage for VM templates.
447	
448	#### Disk Device
449	
450	```hcl
451	// VM Storage Settings
452	vm_disk_device     = "vda"
453	```
454	
455	`vm_disk_device`:`string` - This variable depends on the disk controller used inside of the specific `.auto.pkrvars.hcl` file. By default, the builds use the `virtio-scsi-pci` disk controller and that requires the use of `vda`. If you decide to use a non-virtio controller, then you'll have to change the `vm_disk_device` variable to the appropriate device.
456	
457	#### EFI Device
458	```hcl
459	vm_efi_storage_pool      = "pool0"
460	vm_efi_type              = "4m"
461	vm_efi_pre_enrolled_keys = false
462	```
463	
464	#### Disk Partitions
465	
466	`vm_disk_partitions`:`list[dict]` - Use this list to define the primary partitions that will be created when a specific build runs. Each of the builds process this list in order, so the first partition defined in the list will be the first partition created, the second one listed will be the second one created, and so on.
467	
468	> **Note**
469	>
470	> - All partition sizes are in MegaBytes (MB)
471	> - If you want to have a partition consume all available free space, you can indicate that with `-1`
472	
473	##### Partitioning Examples
474	
475	<details>
476	  <summary>Single Partition Example for BIOS bootloaders</summary>
477	Below is an example of a partition layout for a VM template that boots with BIOS and uses a single partition for the OS.
478	
479	```hcl title="config/linux-storage.pkrvars.hcl"
480	// VM Storage Settings
481	vm_disk_device     = "vda"
482	vm_disk_use_swap   = true
483	vm_disk_partitions = [
484	  {
485	    name = "root"
486	    size = -1,
487	    format = {
488	      label  = "ROOTFS",
489	      fstype = "ext4",
490	    },
491	    mount = {
492	      path    = "/",
493	      options = "",
494	    },
495	    volume_group = "",
496	  },
497	]
498	```
499	</details>
500	
501	<details>
502	  <summary>Single Partition Example for UEFI bootloaders</summary>
503	This example is similar to the above example except that it has the extra partitions needed for the UEFI (OVMF) bootloader. Note the extra variables for the EFI settings.
504	
505	```hcl title="config/linux-storage.pkrvars.hcl"
506	// VM EFI Settings
507	vm_efi_storage_pool      = "pool0"
508	vm_efi_type              = "4m"
509	vm_efi_pre_enrolled_keys = false
510	
511	// VM Storage Settings
512	vm_disk_device     = "vda"
513	vm_disk_use_swap   = true
514	vm_disk_partitions = [
515	  {
516	    name = "efi"
517	    size = 1024,
518	    format = {
519	      label  = "EFIFS",
520	      fstype = "fat32",
521	    },
522	    mount = {
523	      path    = "/boot/efi",
524	      options = "",
525	    },
526	    volume_group = "",
527	  },
528	  {
529	    name = "boot"
530	    size = 1024,
531	    format = {
532	      label  = "BOOTFS",
533	      fstype = "ext4",
534	    },
535	    mount = {
536	      path    = "/boot",
537	      options = "",
538	    },
539	    volume_group = "",
540	  },
541	  {
542	    name = "root"
543	    size = -1,
544	    format = {
545	      label  = "ROOTFS",
546	      fstype = "ext4",
547	    },
548	    mount = {
549	      path    = "/",
550	      options = "",
551	    },
552	    volume_group = "",
553	  },
554	]
555	```
556	</details>
557	
558	<details>
559	  <summary>LVM Partitioning Example with CIS partitions for UEFI bootloaders</summary>
560	This is a more complex example of a partition layout for a VM template that uses LVM and has volumes with mount options required by CIS for hardening a linux system.

562	
563	```hcl title="config/linux-storage.pkrvars.hcl"

583	    },

585	  },
586	  {
587	    name = "boot"

595	      options = "",
596	    },
597	    volume_group = "",
598	  },
599	  {
600	    name = "sysvg"

602	    format = {
603	      label  = "",
604	      fstype = "",
605	    },
606	    mount = {
607	      path    = "",
608	      options = "",
609	    },
610	    volume_group = "sysvg",
611	  },
612	]
613	vm_disk_lvm = [
614	  {
615	    name: "sysvg",
616	    partitions: [
617	      {
618	        name = "lv_swap",
619	        size = 1024,
620	        format = {

623	        },
624	        mount = {
625	          path    = "",
626	          options = "",
627	        },
628	      },
629	      {

631	        size = 10240,
632	        format = {

634	          fstype = "ext4",
635	        },
636	        mount = {
637	          path    = "/",
638	          options = "",

640	      },
641	      {

730	
731	
732	## Packer Machine Image Builds
733	
734	Edit the `<build type>.pkrvars.hcl` file in the `config` directory for each type of build to set the following virtual machine hardware settings, as required:
735	
736	- CPUs `(int)`
737	- CPU Cores `(int)`
738	- Memory in MB `(int)`
739	- Primary Disk in MB `(string)` (e.g. 32GB)
740	- .iso Path `(string)`
741	- .iso File `(string)`
742	
743	```hcl title="config/linux-ubuntu-22-04-lts.pkrvars.hcl"
744	// Guest Operating System Metadata
745	vm_os_language   = "en_US"
746	vm_os_keyboard   = "us"
747	vm_os_timezone   = "UTC"
748	vm_os_family     = "linux"
749	vm_os_name       = "ubuntu"
750	vm_os_version    = "22.04-lts"
751	
752	// Virtual Machine Guest Operating System Setting
753	vm_os_type       = "l26"
754	vm_cloudinit     = true
755	
756	// Virtual Machine Hardware Settings
757	vm_bios                 = "ovmf"
758	vm_cpu_count            = 1
759	vm_cpu_sockets          = 1
760	vm_cpu_type             = "kvm64"
761	vm_mem_size             = 2048
762	vm_disk_type            = "virtio"
763	vm_disk_size            = "32G"
764	vm_disk_format          = "raw"
765	vm_disk_controller_type = "virtio-scsi-pci"
766	vm_network_card_model   = "virtio"
767	
768	// Removable Media Settings
769	iso_path     = "iso"
770	iso_file     = "ubuntu-22.04-live-server-amd64.iso"
771	// The checksum can be a URL or an actual checksum value. URL is preferred
772	iso_checksum = "file:https://releases.ubuntu.com/jammy/SHA256SUMS"
773	
774	// Boot Settings
775	vm_boot      = "order=virtio0;ide2;net0"
776	vm_boot_wait = "5s"
777	
778	// EFI Settings
779	vm_firmware_path         = "./OVMF.fd"
780	```
781	
782	> **Note**
783	>
784	>   All `config/<build type>.pkrvars.hcl` default to using:
785	>   - VirtIO SCSI storage device
786	>   - VirtIO (paravirtualized) network card device
787	>   - UEFI boot firmware
788	
789	The defaults use VirtIO to balance out performance, compatibility, and ease of use. Feel free to change the storage and network controllers to suit your needs. However, if you change the storage or network controllers and run into issues you should change them back to defaults and try the builds again. I won't support any builds that don't use the VirtIO drivers.
790	
791	Both UEFI and BIOS booting are supported for builds. Inside the `<build type>.pkrvars.hcl` file specific to the build, you can set the `vm_bios` variable to either `seabios` for BIOS or `ovmf` for UEFI booting.
792	
793	> **Note**
794	>
795	>   The storage layouts are different for each bootloader type so you'll need to configure the storage layouts accordingly.
796	
797	# Terraform
798	An example Terraform for a UEFI Ubuntu 24.04 template is included.  For more information [go here](example_uefi_ubuntu_terraform/README.md)
799	
800	There is also a script to import an existing vm to use as an example for building Terraform builds.  For more information [go here](terraform_import/README.md)
801	
802	### Cloud-Init
803	All builds for operating systems that support [cloud-init][cloud-init] now have the option to enable it. This can be done on a per-build basis inside the `<build type>.pkrvars.hcl` files in the `config` directory. The default setting is `true`.
804	
805	If a particular linux distribution ships with cloud-init (e.g. Ubuntu) and cloud-init is set to `false` in the `config/<build type>.pkrvars.hcl` file for the build, then cloud-init will be disabled in the operating system **and** within Proxmox for that specific template.
806	
807	You can also change the Cloud-Init storage backend using the `vm_cloudinit_disk_type` variable. See the [Packer Proxmox Integration docs](https://developer.hashicorp.com/packer/integrations/hashicorp/proxmox/latest/components/builder/iso) for more details.
808	
809	# Known Issues
810	
811	## Windows Builds
812	For obvious reasons, product keys and actual ISO names are not provided for any of the Windows builds in this repository. For example, if you wanted to build a Windows 11 template VM, the Windows 11 build uses the Windows 11 Enterprise Eval ISO. By default packer will build the Windows 11 Enterprise Eval whether you choose the Pro or Enterprise build when running `build.sh`. Also, this build by default should not require any interventions by a human.
813	
814	If you have a valid product key and want to build a Windows 11 Pro or Enterprise template VM, you will need to change the following variables (if building Pro you don't need to change Enterprise vars and vice versa):
815	
816	```hcl title="config/windows-desktop-11.pkrvars.hcl"
817	vm_inst_os_image_pro = "Windows 11 Enterprise Evaluation"   <-- If building Professional with a valid key, you will need to enter in whatever the name of the Operating System is when the Windows install prompts you
818	vm_inst_os_key_pro   = "XXXXX-XXXXX-XXXXX-XXXXX-XXXXX"
819	vm_inst_os_image_ent = "Windows 11 Enterprise Evaluation"   <-- If building Enterprise with a valid key, you can remove the Evaluation from this string
820	vm_inst_os_key_ent   = "XXXXX-XXXXX-XXXXX-XXXXX-XXXXX"
821	...
822	iso_file             = "Name-of-Windows-11-Non-Evaluation-DVD.iso"  <-- This is the name of the real Windows 11 DVD
823	```
824	
825	If your template build hangs or times out, re-run it and then open the console of the template VM and see if Windows is waiting for you to select an Operating System. Select the Operating System version and then click Next and the build should work hands-off the rest of the way. If you want this to be completely hands-off, just change the `vm_inst_os_image_pro` and/or `vm_inst_os_image_ent` vars to match the Operating System selection entry exactly. Then the next time you execute the particular Windows template build, it shouldn't get stuck waiting for input.
826	
827	# Unsupported Features
828

830	Contributions are welcome, please read the [CONTRIBUTING](.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) document for more details.

163	  INPUT_PATH="$SCRIPT_PATH"/builds/linux/centos/10-stream/
164	  BUILD_PATH=${INPUT_PATH#"${SCRIPT_PATH}/builds/"}
165	  BUILD_VARS="$(echo "${BUILD_PATH%/}" | tr -s '/' | tr '/' '-').pkrvars.hcl"
166	
167	  echo -e "\nCONFIRM: Build a CentOS 10 Stream Template for Proxmox?"
168	  echo -e "\nContinue? (y/n)"
169	  read -r REPLY
170	  if [[ ! $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]
171	  then
172	    exit 1
173	  fi
174	
175	  ### Build a CentOS 10 Stream Template for Proxmox. ###
176	  echo "Building a CentOS 10 Stream Template for Proxmox..."
177	
178	  ### Initialize HashiCorp Packer and required plugins. ###
179	  echo "Initializing HashiCorp Packer and required plugins..."
180	  packer init "$INPUT_PATH"
181	
182	  ### Start the Build. ###
183	  echo "Starting the build...."
184	  echo "packer build -force -on-error=ask $debug_option"
185	  packer build -force -on-error=ask $debug_option \
186	      -var-file="$CONFIG_PATH/ansible.pkrvars.hcl" \
187	      -var-file="$CONFIG_PATH/build.pkrvars.hcl" \
188	      -var-file="$CONFIG_PATH/common.pkrvars.hcl" \
189	      -var-file="$CONFIG_PATH/linux-storage.pkrvars.hcl" \
190	      -var-file="$CONFIG_PATH/network.pkrvars.hcl" \
191	      -var-file="$CONFIG_PATH/proxmox.pkrvars.hcl" \
192	      -var-file="$CONFIG_PATH/$BUILD_VARS" \
193	      "$INPUT_PATH"
194	
195	  ### All done. ###
196	  echo "Done."
197	}

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	set -e
3	
4	# Load common functions
5	source common.sh
6	
7	# === Colors ===
8	GREEN="\033[0;32m"
9	YELLOW="\033[1;33m"
10	RED="\033[0;31m"
11	BLUE="\033[0;34m"
12	NC="\033[0m"
13	
14	# === Paths ===
15	SCRIPT_PATH=$(realpath "$(dirname "$(follow_link "$0")")")
16	CONFIG_PATH=$(realpath "${1:-${SCRIPT_PATH}/config}")
17	
18	INPUT_PATHS=(
19	  "$SCRIPT_PATH/builds/linux/almalinux/10/"
20	  "$SCRIPT_PATH/builds/linux/almalinux/9/"
21	  "$SCRIPT_PATH/builds/linux/almalinux/8/"
22	  "$SCRIPT_PATH/builds/linux/centos/10-stream/"
23	  "$SCRIPT_PATH/builds/linux/centos/9-stream/"
24	  "$SCRIPT_PATH/builds/linux/debian/12/"
25	  "$SCRIPT_PATH/builds/linux/debian/11/"
26	  "$SCRIPT_PATH/builds/linux/opensuse/leap-15-6/"
27	  "$SCRIPT_PATH/builds/linux/opensuse/leap-15-5/"
28	  "$SCRIPT_PATH/builds/linux/oracle/9/"
29	  "$SCRIPT_PATH/builds/linux/oracle/8/"
30	  "$SCRIPT_PATH/builds/linux/rocky/10/"
31	  "$SCRIPT_PATH/builds/linux/rocky/9/"
32	  "$SCRIPT_PATH/builds/linux/rocky/8/"
33	  "$SCRIPT_PATH/builds/linux/ubuntu/24-04-lts/"
34	  "$SCRIPT_PATH/builds/linux/ubuntu/22-04-lts/"
35	  "$SCRIPT_PATH/builds/linux/ubuntu/20-04-lts/"
36	  "$SCRIPT_PATH/builds/windows/desktop/11/"
37	)
38	
39	# === Result storage ===
40	declare -a summary=()
41	
42	# === Validation Function ===
43	validate_packer() {
44	  local input_path="$1"
45	  local current_build_path current_build_vars os version
46	
47	  version=$(basename "$input_path")
48	  os=$(basename "$(dirname "$input_path")")
49	
50	  echo -e "${BLUE}▶ Validating: ${YELLOW}${os^^} ${version}${NC}"
51	
52	  if echo "$input_path" | grep -qi "windows"; then
53	    current_build_path=${input_path#"${SCRIPT_PATH}/builds/"}
54	    current_build_vars="$(echo "${current_build_path%/}" | tr -s '/' | tr '/' '-').pkrvars.hcl"
55	    VAR_FILES=(
56	      "$CONFIG_PATH/ansible.pkrvars.hcl"
57	      "$CONFIG_PATH/build.pkrvars.hcl"
58	      "$CONFIG_PATH/common.pkrvars.hcl"
59	      "$CONFIG_PATH/network.pkrvars.hcl"
60	      "$CONFIG_PATH/proxmox.pkrvars.hcl"
61	      "$CONFIG_PATH/proxy.pkrvars.hcl"
62	      "$CONFIG_PATH/$current_build_vars"
63	    )
64	  else
65	    current_build_path=${input_path#"${SCRIPT_PATH}/builds/"}
66	    current_build_vars="$(echo "${current_build_path%/}" | tr -s '/' | tr '/' '-').pkrvars.hcl"
67	    VAR_FILES=(
68	      "$CONFIG_PATH/ansible.pkrvars.hcl"
69	      "$CONFIG_PATH/build.pkrvars.hcl"
70	      "$CONFIG_PATH/common.pkrvars.hcl"
71	      "$CONFIG_PATH/linux-storage.pkrvars.hcl"
72	      "$CONFIG_PATH/network.pkrvars.hcl"
73	      "$CONFIG_PATH/proxmox.pkrvars.hcl"
74	      "$CONFIG_PATH/proxy.pkrvars.hcl"
75	      "$CONFIG_PATH/$current_build_vars"
76	    )
77	  fi
78	
79	if packer_output=$(packer validate "${VAR_FILES[@]/#/--var-file=}" "$input_path" 2>&1); then
80	  echo -e "  ${GREEN}✔ PASS${NC}"
81	  summary+=("${os}/${version} : PASS")
82	else
83	  echo -e "  ${RED}✘ FAIL${NC}"
84	  echo "$packer_output"
85	  summary+=("${os}/${version} : FAIL")
86	fi
87	
88	  echo -e "${NC}----------------------------------------"
89	}
90	
91	# === Main Execution ===
92	echo -e "${BLUE}==> Starting Packer validations...${NC}"
93	for path in "${INPUT_PATHS[@]}"; do
94	  validate_packer "$path"
95	done
96	
97	# === Summary Output ===
98	echo -e "\n${BLUE}==> Validation Summary:${NC}"
99	for entry in "${summary[@]}"; do
100	  if [[ "$entry" == *FAIL ]]; then
101	    echo -e "${RED}$entry${NC}"
102	  else
103	    echo -e "${GREEN}$entry${NC}"
104	  fi
105	done
106	
107
```

1	# Use the base Ubuntu devcontainer image.
2	FROM mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/base:ubuntu
3	
4	# Install additional packages.
5	RUN apt-get update && \
6	    apt-get install -y curl software-properties-common ca-certificates
7	
8	RUN update-ca-certificates
9	
10	# Install Packer
11	RUN sudo mkdir -m 0755 -p /etc/apt/keyrings/ && \ 
12	    curl -fsSL https://apt.releases.hashicorp.com/gpg | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/hashicorp-packer.gpg && \
13	    sudo echo "deb [arch=amd64, signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/hashicorp-packer.gpg] https://apt.releases.hashicorp.com $(lsb_release -cs) main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/hashicorp-packer.list > /dev/null && \
14	    sudo chmod 0644 /etc/apt/keyrings/hashicorp-packer.gpg && \
15	    sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install packer
16	
17	# Install Python3 and Ansible.
18	RUN add-apt-repository --yes --update ppa:ansible/ansible && \
19	    apt-get update && \
20	    apt-get install -y python3 python3-pip ansible
21	
22	# Cleanup.
23	RUN apt-get autoremove -y && \
24	    apt-get clean && \
25	    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
26	
27	# Set PATH
28	ENV PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"
```

1	follow_link() {
2		FILE="$1"
3		while [ -h "$FILE" ]; do
4			# On macOS, readlink -f doesn't work.
5			FILE="$(readlink "$FILE")"
6		done
7		echo "$FILE"
8	}
9	
10

1	/*
2	    DESCRIPTION:
3	    Ubuntu Server 24.04 LTS template using the Packer Builder for Proxmox (proxmox-iso).
4	*/
5	
6	//  BLOCK: packer
7	//  The Packer configuration.
8	
9	packer {
10	  required_version = ">= 1.12.0"
11	  required_plugins {
12	    ansible = {
13	      source  = "github.com/hashicorp/ansible"
14	      version = "~> 1"
15	    }
16	    git = {
17	      version = ">= 0.6.2"
18	      source  = "github.com/ethanmdavidson/git"
19	    }
20	    proxmox = {
21	      version = "= 1.2.1"
22	      source  = "github.com/hashicorp/proxmox"
23	    }
24	  }
25	}
26	
27	//  BLOCK: data
28	//  Defines the data sources.
29	
30	data "git-repository" "cwd" {}
31	
32	//  BLOCK: locals
33	//  Defines the local variables.
34	
35	locals {
36	  bios_boot_command = [
37	    "c<wait5>",
38	    "linux /casper/vmlinuz --- autoinstall ${local.data_source_command}",
39	    "<enter><wait10>",
40	    "initrd /casper/initrd",
41	    "<enter><wait10>",
42	    "boot",
43	    "<enter>"
44	  ]
45	  uefi_boot_command = [
46	    // This waits for 3 seconds, sends the "c" key, and then waits for another 3 seconds. In the GRUB boot loader, this is used to enter command line mode.
47	    "<wait3s>c<wait3s>",
48	    // This types a command to load the Linux kernel from the specified path with the 'autoinstall' option and the value of the 'data_source_command' local variable.
49	    // The 'autoinstall' option is used to automate the installation process.
50	    // The 'data_source_command' local variable is used to specify the kickstart data source configured in the common variables.
51	    "linux /casper/vmlinuz --- autoinstall ${local.data_source_command}",
52	    // This sends the "enter" key and then waits. This is typically used to execute the command and give the system time to process it.
53	    "<enter><wait>",
54	    // This types a command to load the initial RAM disk from the specified path.
55	    "initrd /casper/initrd",
56	    // This sends the "enter" key and then waits. This is typically used to execute the command and give the system time to process it.
57	    "<enter><wait>",
58	    // This types the "boot" command. This starts the boot process using the loaded kernel and initial RAM disk.
59	    "boot",
60	    // This sends the "enter" key. This is typically used to execute the command.
61	    "<enter>"
62	  ]
63	  build_by          = "Built by: HashiCorp Packer ${packer.version}"
64	  build_date        = formatdate("DD-MM-YYYY hh:mm ZZZ", "${timestamp()}" )
65	  build_version     = data.git-repository.cwd.head
66	  build_description = "Version: ${local.build_version}\nBuilt on: ${local.build_date}\n${local.build_by}\nCloud-Init: ${var.vm_cloudinit}"
67	  vm_disk_type      = var.vm_disk_type == "virtio" ? "vda" : "sda"
68	  manifest_date     = formatdate("YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss", timestamp())
69	  manifest_path     = "${path.cwd}/manifests/"
70	  manifest_output   = "${local.manifest_path}${local.manifest_date}.json"
71	  data_source_content = {
72	    "/meta-data" = file("${abspath(path.root)}/data/meta-data")
73	    "/user-data" = templatefile("${abspath(path.root)}/data/user-data.pkrtpl.hcl", {
74	      build_username           = var.build_username
75	      build_password           = var.build_password
76	      build_password_encrypted = var.build_password_encrypted
77	      vm_disk_type             = local.vm_disk_type
78	      vm_os_language           = var.vm_os_language
79	      vm_os_keyboard           = var.vm_os_keyboard
80	      vm_os_timezone           = var.vm_os_timezone
81	
82	      network                  = templatefile("${abspath(path.root)}/data/network.pkrtpl.hcl", {
83	        device                 = var.vm_network_device
84	        ip                     = var.vm_ip_address
85	        netmask                = var.vm_ip_netmask
86	        gateway                = var.vm_ip_gateway
87	        dns                    = var.vm_dns_list
88	      })
89	      storage                  = templatefile("${abspath(path.root)}/data/storage.pkrtpl.hcl", {
90	        device                 = var.vm_disk_device
91	        swap                   = var.vm_disk_use_swap
92	        partitions             = var.vm_disk_partitions
93	        lvm                    = var.vm_disk_lvm
94	        vm_bios                = var.vm_bios
95	      })
96	      additional_packages = var.additional_packages
97	    })
98	  }
99	  data_source_command = var.common_data_source == "http" ? "ds=\"nocloud-net;seedfrom=http://{{.HTTPIP}}:{{.HTTPPort}}/\"" : "ds=\"nocloud\""
100	  vm_name = "${var.vm_os_family}-${var.vm_os_name}-${var.vm_os_version}"
101	  boot_command = var.vm_bios == "ovmf" ? local.uefi_boot_command : local.bios_boot_command
102	  vm_bios = var.vm_bios == "ovmf" ? var.vm_firmware_path : null
103	}
104	
105	//  BLOCK: source
106	//  Defines the builder configuration blocks.
107	
108	source "proxmox-iso" "ubuntu" {
109	
110	  // Proxmox Connection Settings and Credentials
111	  proxmox_url              = "https://${var.proxmox_hostname}:8006/api2/json"
112	  username                 = "${var.proxmox_api_token_id}"
113	  token                    = "${var.proxmox_api_token_secret}"
114	  insecure_skip_tls_verify = "${var.proxmox_insecure_connection}"
115	
116	  // Proxmox Settings
117	  node                     = "${var.proxmox_node}"
118	
119	  // Virtual Machine Settings
120	  vm_name         = "${local.vm_name}"
121	  bios            = "${var.vm_bios}"
122	  sockets         = "${var.vm_cpu_sockets}"
123	  cores           = "${var.vm_cpu_count}"
124	  cpu_type        = "${var.vm_cpu_type}"
125	  memory          = "${var.vm_mem_size}"
126	  os              = "${var.vm_os_type}"
127	  scsi_controller = "${var.vm_disk_controller_type}"
128	  vm_id           = var.vm_id_number
129	  
130	  disks {
131	    disk_size     = "${var.vm_disk_size}"
132	    type          = "${var.vm_disk_type}"
133	    storage_pool  = "${var.vm_storage_pool}"
134	    format        = "${var.vm_disk_format}"
135	  }
136	
137	  dynamic "efi_config" {
138	    for_each = var.vm_bios == "ovmf" ? [1] : []
139	    content {
140	      efi_storage_pool  = var.vm_bios == "ovmf" ? var.vm_efi_storage_pool : null
141	      efi_type          = var.vm_bios == "ovmf" ? var.vm_efi_type : null
142	      pre_enrolled_keys = var.vm_bios == "ovmf" ? var.vm_efi_pre_enrolled_keys : null
143	    }
144	  }
145	
146	  ssh_username    = "${var.build_username}"
147	  ssh_password    = "${var.build_password}"
148	  ssh_timeout     = "${var.timeout}"
149	  ssh_port        = "22"
150	  qemu_agent      = true
151	
152	  network_adapters {
153	    bridge     = "${var.vm_bridge_interface}"
154	    model      = "${var.vm_network_card_model}"
155	    vlan_tag   = "${var.vm_vlan_tag}"
156	  }
157	
158	  // Removable Media Settings
159	  http_content = "${var.common_data_source}" == "http" ? "${local.data_source_content}" : null
160	
161	  // Boot and Provisioning Settings
162	  http_interface    = var.common_data_source == "http" ? var.common_http_interface : null
163	  http_bind_address = var.common_data_source == "http" ? var.common_http_bind_address : null
164	  http_port_min     = var.common_data_source == "http" ? var.common_http_port_min : null
165	  http_port_max     = var.common_data_source == "http" ? var.common_http_port_max : null
166	  boot              = var.vm_boot
167	  boot_wait         = var.vm_boot_wait
168	  boot_command      = local.boot_command
169	
170	  boot_iso {
171	    iso_file      = "${var.common_iso_storage}:${var.iso_path}/${var.iso_file}"
172	    unmount       = true
173	    iso_checksum  = "${var.iso_checksum}"
174	  }
175	
176	  dynamic "additional_iso_files" {
177	    for_each = var.common_data_source == "disk" ? [1] : []
178	    content {
179	      cd_files = var.common_data_source == "disk" ? local.data_source_content : null
180	      cd_label = var.common_data_source == "disk" ? "cidata" : null
181	      iso_storage_pool = var.common_data_source == "disk" ? "local" : null
182	    }
183	  }
184	
185	  template_name        = "${local.vm_name}"
186	  template_description = "${local.build_description}"
187	
188	  # VM Cloud Init Settings
189	  cloud_init              = var.vm_cloudinit
190	  cloud_init_storage_pool = var.vm_cloudinit == true ? var.vm_storage_pool : null
191	  cloud_init_disk_type = var.vm_cloudinit_disk_type
192	
193	
194	}
195	
196	# Build Definition to create the VM Template
197	build {
198	  sources = ["source.proxmox-iso.ubuntu"]
199	
200	  provisioner "ansible" {
201	    user                   = var.build_username
202	    galaxy_file            = "${path.cwd}/ansible/linux-requirements.yml"
203	    galaxy_force_with_deps = true
204	    playbook_file          = "${path.cwd}/ansible/linux-playbook.yml"
205	    roles_path             = "${path.cwd}/ansible/roles"
206	    ansible_env_vars = [
207	      "ANSIBLE_CONFIG=${path.cwd}/ansible/ansible.cfg",
208	      "ANSIBLE_PYTHON_INTERPRETER=/usr/bin/python3"
209	    ]
210	    extra_arguments = [
211	      "--extra-vars", "display_skipped_hosts=false",
212	      "--extra-vars", "build_username=${var.build_username}",
213	      "--extra-vars", "build_key='${var.build_key}'",
214	      "--extra-vars", "ansible_username=${var.ansible_username}",
215	      "--extra-vars", "ansible_key='${var.ansible_key}'",
216	      "--extra-vars", "enable_cloudinit='${var.vm_cloudinit}'",
217	    ]
218	  }
219	
220	  post-processor "manifest" {
221	    output     = local.manifest_output
222	    strip_path = true
223	    strip_time = true
224	    custom_data = {
225	      ansible_username         = "${var.ansible_username}"
226	      build_username           = "${var.build_username}"
227	      build_date               = "${local.build_date}"
228	      build_version            = "${local.build_version}"
229	      common_data_source       = "${var.common_data_source}"
230	      vm_cpu_sockets           = "${var.vm_cpu_sockets}"
231	      vm_cpu_count             = "${var.vm_cpu_count}"
232	      vm_disk_size             = "${var.vm_disk_size}"
233	      vm_bios                  = "${var.vm_bios}"
234	      vm_os_type               = "${var.vm_os_type}"
235	      vm_mem_size              = "${var.vm_mem_size}"
236	      vm_network_card_model    = "${var.vm_network_card_model}"
237	      vm_cloudinit             = "${var.vm_cloudinit}"
238	    }
239	  }
240	}

## Review Summary

This is a Packer-based project for building VM templates on Proxmox. Here's what I'll help you with:

- Installs Packer, Ansible, and all dependencies

- Works consistently across environments

### 2. **Ubuntu 26.04 Support**

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```
# Production Dockerfile for Packer Proxmox Builds
FROM ubuntu:24.04

# Set environment variables
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive \
    PACKER_VERSION=1.12.0 \
    ANSIBLE_VERSION=2.17 \
    PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"

# Install base dependencies
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y \
    curl \
    wget \
    gpg \
    software-properties-common \
    ca-certificates \
    git \
    openssh-client \
    jq \
    unzip \
    && update-ca-certificates

# Install Packer
RUN mkdir -m 0755 -p /etc/apt/keyrings/ && \
    curl -fsSL https://apt.releases.hashicorp.com/gpg | gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/hashicorp-packer.gpg && \
    echo "deb [arch=amd64 signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/hashicorp-packer.gpg] https://apt.releases.hashicorp.com $(grep VERSION_CODENAME /etc/os-release | cut -d= -f2) main" | tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/hashicorp-packer.list > /dev/null && \
    chmod 0644 /etc/apt/keyrings/hashicorp-packer.gpg && \
    apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y packer

# Install Python3 and Ansible
RUN add-apt-repository --yes --update ppa:ansible/ansible && \
    apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y \
    python3 \
    python3-pip \
    python3-venv \
    ansible-core \
    && ansible-galaxy collection install ansible.posix community.general

# Cleanup
RUN apt-get autoremove -y && \
    apt-get clean && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Create working directory
WORKDIR /workspace

# Create config directory that can be mounted
VOLUME ["/workspace/config"]

# Copy project files (can be overridden with volume mount)
COPY . /workspace/

# Set default command
CMD ["/bin/bash"]

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```yaml
version: '3.8'

services:
  packer:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    image: oncall-packer:latest
    container_name: oncall-packer
    volumes:
      # Mount the entire project directory
      - .:/workspace
      # Mount config directory for easy configuration management
      - ./config:/workspace/config
      # Mount manifests directory to persist build outputs
      - ./manifests:/workspace/manifests
      # Mount SSH keys if needed for build authentication
      - ~/.ssh:/root/.ssh:ro
    working_dir: /workspace
    environment:
      - PACKER_LOG=${PACKER_LOG:-0}
      - PACKER_LOG_PATH=${PACKER_LOG_PATH:-/workspace/packer.log}
    network_mode: host
    stdin_open: true
    tty: true
    command: /bin/bash

  # Alternative service for running builds non-interactively
  packer-build:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    image: oncall-packer:latest
    volumes:
      - .:/workspace
      - ./config:/workspace/config
      - ./manifests:/workspace/manifests
      - ~/.ssh:/root/.ssh:ro
    working_dir: /workspace
    environment:
      - PACKER_LOG=${PACKER_LOG:-0}
      - PACKER_LOG_PATH=${PACKER_LOG_PATH:-/workspace/packer.log}
    network_mode: host
    profiles:
      - build

```

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 2026-05-07 16:14:49Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```hcl
/*
    DESCRIPTION:
    Ubuntu Server 26.04 LTS template using the Packer Builder for Proxmox (proxmox-iso).
*/

//  BLOCK: packer
//  The Packer configuration.

packer {
  required_version = ">= 1.12.0"
  required_plugins {
    ansible = {
      source  = "github.com/hashicorp/ansible"
      version = "~> 1"
    }
    git = {
      version = ">= 0.6.2"
      source  = "github.com/ethanmdavidson/git"
    }
    proxmox = {
      version = "= 1.2.1"
      source  = "github.com/hashicorp/proxmox"
    }
  }
}

//  BLOCK: data
//  Defines the data sources.

data "git-repository" "cwd" {}

//  BLOCK: locals
//  Defines the local variables.

locals {
  bios_boot_command = [
    "c<wait5>",
    "linux /casper/vmlinuz --- autoinstall ${local.data_source_command}",
    "<enter><wait10>",
    "initrd /casper/initrd",
    "<enter><wait10>",
    "boot",
    "<enter>"
  ]
  uefi_boot_command = [
    // This waits for 3 seconds, sends the "c" key, and then waits for another 3 seconds. In the GRUB boot loader, this is used to enter command line mode.
    "<wait3s>c<wait3s>",
    // This types a command to load the Linux kernel from the specified path with the 'autoinstall' option and the value of the 'data_source_command' local variable.
    // The 'autoinstall' option is used to automate the installation process.
    // The 'data_source_command' local variable is used to specify the kickstart data source configured in the common variables.
    "linux /casper/vmlinuz --- autoinstall ${local.data_source_command}",
    // This sends the "enter" key and then waits. This is typically used to execute the command and give the system time to process it.
    "<enter><wait>",
    // This types a command to load the initial RAM disk from the specified path.
    "initrd /casper/initrd",
    // This sends the "enter" key and then waits. This is typically used to execute the command and give the system time to process it.
    "<enter><wait>",
    // This types the "boot" command. This starts the boot process using the loaded kernel and initial RAM disk.
    "boot",
    // This sends the "enter" key. This is typically used to execute the command.
    "<enter>"
  ]
  build_by          = "Built by: HashiCorp Packer ${packer.version}"
  build_date        = formatdate("DD-MM-YYYY hh:mm ZZZ", "${timestamp()}" )
  build_version     = data.git-repository.cwd.head
  build_description = "Version: ${local.build_version}\nBuilt on: ${local.build_date}\n${local.build_by}\nCloud-Init: ${var.vm_cloudinit}"
  vm_disk_type      = var.vm_disk_type == "virtio" ? "vda" : "sda"
  manifest_date     = formatdate("YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss", timestamp())
  manifest_path     = "${path.cwd}/manifests/"
  manifest_output   = "${local.manifest_path}${local.manifest_date}.json"
  data_source_content = {
    "/meta-data" = file("${abspath(path.root)}/data/meta-data")
    "/user-data" = templatefile("${abspath(path.root)}/data/user-data.pkrtpl.hcl", {
      build_username           = var.build_username
      build_password           = var.build_password
      build_password_encrypted = var.build_password_encrypted
      vm_disk_type             = local.vm_disk_type
      vm_os_language           = var.vm_os_language
      vm_os_keyboard           = var.vm_os_keyboard
      vm_os_timezone           = var.vm_os_timezone

      network                  = templatefile("${abspath(path.root)}/data/network.pkrtpl.hcl", {
        device                 = var.vm_network_device
        ip                     = var.vm_ip_address
        netmask                = var.vm_ip_netmask
        gateway                = var.vm_ip_gateway
        dns                    = var.vm_dns_list
      })
      storage                  = templatefile("${abspath(path.root)}/data/storage.pkrtpl.hcl", {
        device                 = var.vm_disk_device
        swap                   = var.vm_disk_use_swap
        partitions             = var.vm_disk_partitions
        lvm                    = var.vm_disk_lvm
        vm_bios                = var.vm_bios
      })
      additional_packages = var.additional_packages
    })
  }
  data_source_command = var.common_data_source == "http" ? "ds=\"nocloud-net;seedfrom=http://{{.HTTPIP}}:{{.HTTPPort}}/\"" : "ds=\"nocloud\""
  vm_name = "${var.vm_os_family}-${var.vm_os_name}-${var.vm_os_version}"
  boot_command = var.vm_bios == "ovmf" ? local.uefi_boot_command : local.bios_boot_command
  vm_bios = var.vm_bios == "ovmf" ? var.vm_firmware_path : null
}

//  BLOCK: source
//  Defines the builder configuration blocks.

source "proxmox-iso" "ubuntu" {

  // Proxmox Connection Settings and Credentials
  proxmox_url              = "https://${var.proxmox_hostname}:8006/api2/json"
  username                 = "${var.proxmox_api_token_id}"
  token                    = "${var.proxmox_api_token_secret}"
  insecure_skip_tls_verify = "${var.proxmox_insecure_connection}"

  // Proxmox Settings
  node                     = "${var.proxmox_node}"

  // Virtual Machine Settings
  vm_name         = "${local.vm_name}"
  bios            = "${var.vm_bios}"
  sockets         = "${var.vm_cpu_sockets}"
  cores           = "${var.vm_cpu_count}"
  cpu_type        = "${var.vm_cpu_type}"
  memory          = "${var.vm_mem_size}"
  os              = "${var.vm_os_type}"
  scsi_controller = "${var.vm_disk_controller_type}"
  vm_id           = var.vm_id_number

  disks {
    disk_size     = "${var.vm_disk_size}"
    type          = "${var.vm_disk_type}"
    storage_pool  = "${var.vm_storage_pool}"
    format        = "${var.vm_disk_format}"
  }

  dynamic "efi_config" {
    for_each = var.vm_bios == "ovmf" ? [1] : []
    content {
      efi_storage_pool  = var.vm_bios == "ovmf" ? var.vm_efi_storage_pool : null
      efi_type          = var.vm_bios == "ovmf" ? var.vm_efi_type : null
      pre_enrolled_keys = var.vm_bios == "ovmf" ? var.vm_efi_pre_enrolled_keys : null
    }
  }

  ssh_username    = "${var.build_username}"
  ssh_password    = "${var.build_password}"
  ssh_timeout     = "${var.timeout}"
  ssh_port        = "22"
  qemu_agent      = true

  network_adapters {
    bridge     = "${var.vm_bridge_interface}"
    model      = "${var.vm_network_card_model}"
    vlan_tag   = "${var.vm_vlan_tag}"
  }

  // Removable Media Settings
  http_content = "${var.common_data_source}" == "http" ? "${local.data_source_content}" : null

  // Boot and Provisioning Settings
  http_interface    = var.common_data_source == "http" ? var.common_http_interface : null
  http_bind_address = var.common_data_source == "http" ? var.common_http_bind_address : null
  http_port_min     = var.common_data_source == "http" ? var.common_http_port_min : null
  http_port_max     = var.common_data_source == "http" ? var.common_http_port_max : null
  boot              = var.vm_boot
  boot_wait         = var.vm_boot_wait
  boot_command      = local.boot_command

  boot_iso {
    iso_file      = "${var.common_iso_storage}:${var.iso_path}/${var.iso_file}"
    unmount       = true
    iso_checksum  = "${var.iso_checksum}"
  }

  dynamic "additional_iso_files" {
    for_each = var.common_data_source == "disk" ? [1] : []
    content {
      cd_files = var.common_data_source == "disk" ? local.data_source_content : null
      cd_label = var.common_data_source == "disk" ? "cidata" : null
      iso_storage_pool = var.common_data_source == "disk" ? "local" : null
    }
  }

  template_name        = "${local.vm_name}"
  template_description = "${local.build_description}"

  # VM Cloud Init Settings
  cloud_init              = var.vm_cloudinit
  cloud_init_storage_pool = var.vm_cloudinit == true ? var.vm_storage_pool : null
  cloud_init_disk_type = var.vm_cloudinit_disk_type


}

# Build Definition to create the VM Template
build {
  sources = ["source.proxmox-iso.ubuntu"]

  provisioner "ansible" {
    user                   = var.build_username
    galaxy_file            = "${path.cwd}/ansible/linux-requirements.yml"
    galaxy_force_with_deps = true
    playbook_file          = "${path.cwd}/ansible/linux-playbook.yml"
    roles_path             = "${path.cwd}/ansible/roles"
    ansible_env_vars = [
      "ANSIBLE_CONFIG=${path.cwd}/ansible/ansible.cfg",
      "ANSIBLE_PYTHON_INTERPRETER=/usr/bin/python3"
    ]
    extra_arguments = [
      "--extra-vars", "display_skipped_hosts=false",
      "--extra-vars", "build_username=${var.build_username}",
      "--extra-vars", "build_key='${var.build_key}'",
      "--extra-vars", "ansible_username=${var.ansible_username}",
      "--extra-vars", "ansible_key='${var.ansible_key}'",
      "--extra-vars", "enable_cloudinit='${var.vm_cloudinit}'",
    ]
  }

  post-processor "manifest" {
    output     = local.manifest_output
    strip_path = true
    strip_time = true
    custom_data = {
      ansible_username         = "${var.ansible_username}"
      build_username           = "${var.build_username}"
      build_date               = "${local.build_date}"
      build_version            = "${local.build_version}"
      common_data_source       = "${var.common_data_source}"
      vm_cpu_sockets           = "${var.vm_cpu_sockets}"
      vm_cpu_count             = "${var.vm_cpu_count}"
      vm_disk_size             = "${var.vm_disk_size}"
      vm_bios                  = "${var.vm_bios}"
      vm_os_type               = "${var.vm_os_type}"
      vm_mem_size              = "${var.vm_mem_size}"
      vm_network_card_model    = "${var.vm_network_card_model}"
      vm_cloudinit             = "${var.vm_cloudinit}"
    }
  }
}

17	## Introduction
18	
19	This repository provides opinionated infrastructure-as-code examples to automate the creation of virtual machine images and their guest operating systems on Proxmox using [HashiCorp Packer][packer] and the [Packer Plugin for Proxmox][packer-plugin-proxmox] (`proxmox-iso` builder). All examples are authored in the HashiCorp Configuration Language ("HCL2").
20	
21	By default, the machine image artifacts are converted to templates within Proxmox after a virtual machine is built and configured according to the individual templates.
22

```md
# Docker Usage Guide

This guide explains how to use the containerized version of this Packer project.

## Quick Start

### Build the Docker Image

\```bash
docker-compose build
\```

### Run Interactively

\```bash
docker-compose run --rm packer
\```

This will drop you into a bash shell inside the container with all tools installed.

### Run a Specific Build

From inside the container:

\```bash
./build.sh
\```

Then select your desired build from the menu.

### Run with Debug Mode

\```bash
docker-compose run --rm packer ./build.sh --debug
\```

## Configuration

### Mounting Config Directory

The `config` directory is automatically mounted as a volume. This allows you to:

1. **Create your configuration on the host:**
   \```bash
   ./config.sh
   \```

2. **Edit configuration files on your host machine** using your favorite editor

3. **Run builds in the container** with your host configuration:
   \```bash
   docker-compose run --rm packer ./build.sh
   \```

### Persisting Build Artifacts

The `manifests` directory is also mounted as a volume, so all build manifests will persist on your host machine even after the container is removed.

## Advanced Usage

### Custom Config Path

To use a different config directory:

\```bash
docker-compose run --rm packer ./build.sh /workspace/my-custom-config
\```

### SSH Keys

If your builds require SSH keys for authentication, they're automatically mounted read-only from `~/.ssh` on your host.

### Environment Variables

Set Packer logging:

\```bash
PACKER_LOG=1 docker-compose run --rm packer ./build.sh
\```

Or add to `docker-compose.yml`:

\```yaml
environment:
  - PACKER_LOG=1
  - PACKER_LOG_PATH=/workspace/packer.log
\```

### Validation

Validate all Packer templates:

\```bash
docker-compose run --rm packer ./validate.sh
\```

### Network Mode

The container uses `host` network mode by default to ensure Packer can communicate with your Proxmox server and serve HTTP content for kickstart files. If you need different networking, modify the `network_mode` in `docker-compose.yml`.

## Building Without Docker Compose

If you prefer not to use docker-compose:

### Build the image:

\```bash
docker build -t oncall-packer:latest .
\```

### Run interactively:

\```bash
docker run -it --rm \
  --network host \
  -v "$(pwd):/workspace" \
  -v "$(pwd)/config:/workspace/config" \
  -v "$(pwd)/manifests:/workspace/manifests" \
  -v "$HOME/.ssh:/root/.ssh:ro" \
  oncall-packer:latest
\```

### Run a single command:

\```bash
docker run -it --rm \
  --network host \
  -v "$(pwd):/workspace" \
  -v "$(pwd)/config:/workspace/config" \
  oncall-packer:latest \
  ./validate.sh
\```

## Troubleshooting

### Permission Issues

If you encounter permission issues with mounted volumes, you may need to adjust ownership:

\```bash
sudo chown -R $(id -u):$(id -g) config/ manifests/
\```

### Proxmox Connection Issues

Ensure:
1. The container can reach your Proxmox server (check `proxmox_hostname` in config)
2. Network mode is set to `host` in docker-compose.yml
3. Your firewall allows the HTTP server ports (default 8000-8099)

### Packer Plugin Downloads

On first run, Packer will download required plugins. This may take a few minutes. If you're behind a proxy, set appropriate proxy environment variables in docker-compose.yml.

## Benefits of Using Docker

1. **Consistent Environment**: Same Packer, Ansible, and dependency versions across all systems
2. **Portability**: Works on Windows, macOS, and Linux without installing dependencies
3. **Isolation**: Doesn't pollute your host system with tools
4. **Easy Updates**: Rebuild the image to update all tools to latest versions
5. **Team Collaboration**: Everyone uses the same environment via the Dockerfile

## CI/CD Integration

### GitHub Actions Example

\```yaml
name: Validate Packer Templates

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  validate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Build Docker Image
        run: docker-compose build
      
      - name: Validate Templates
        run: docker-compose run --rm packer ./validate.sh
\```

### GitLab CI Example

\```yaml
validate:
  image: docker:latest
  services:
    - docker:dind
  script:
    - docker-compose build
    - docker-compose run --rm packer ./validate.sh
\```

```

48	## Requirements
49	
50	**Operating Systems**:
51	
52	Operating systems and versions tested with the project:
53	
54	- Proxmox PVE Version >= 8
55	- Ubuntu Server 22.04 LTS (`x86_64`)
56	- CentOS Stream 9 (`x86_64`)
57	
58	**Packer**:
59	
60	- HashiCorp [Packer][packer-install] 1.12.0 or higher.
61	
62	  > **Note**
63	  >
64	  > Click on the operating system name to display the installation steps.
65	
66	  - <details>
67	      <summary>Ubuntu</summary>
68	
69	    The Terraform packages are signed using a private key controlled by HashiCorp, so you must configure your system to trust that HashiCorp key for package authentication.
70	
71	    To configure your repository:
72	
73	    ```shell
74	    sudo bash -c 'wget -O- https://apt.releases.hashicorp.com/gpg | gpg --dearmor > /usr/share/keyrings/hashicorp-archive-keyring.gpg'
75	    ```
76	
77	    Verify the key's fingerprint:
78	
79	    ```shell
80	    gpg --no-default-keyring --keyring /usr/share/keyrings/hashicorp-archive-keyring.gpg --fingerprint
81	    ```
82	
83	    The fingerprint must match E8A0 32E0 94D8 EB4E A189 D270 DA41 8C88 A321 9F7B. You can also verify the key on [Security at HashiCorp][hcp-security] under Linux Package Checksum Verification.
84	
85	    Add the official HashiCorp repository to your system:
86	
87	    ```shell
88	    sudo bash -c 'echo "deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/hashicorp-archive-keyring.gpg] \
89	    https://apt.releases.hashicorp.com $(lsb_release -cs) main" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/hashicorp.list'
90	    ```
91	
92	    Install Packer from HashiCorp repository:
93	
94	    ```shell
95	    sudo apt update && sudo apt install packer
96	    ```
97	
98	    </details>
99	
100	  - <details>
101	      <summary>CentOS Stream 9</summary>
102	
103	    Install `yum-config-manager` to manage your repositories.
104	
105	    ```shell
106	    sudo yum install -y yum-utils
107	    ```
108	
109	    Use `yum-config-manager` to add the official HashiCorp Linux repository:
110	
111	    ```shell
112	    sudo yum-config-manager --add-repo https://rpm.releases.hashicorp.com/RHEL/hashicorp.repo
113	    ```
114	
115	    Install.
116	
117	    ```shell
118	    sudo yum -y install packer
119	    ```
120	    </details>
121	
122	
123	- Packer plugins:
124	
125	  > **Note**
126	  >
127	  > Required plugins are automatically downloaded and initialized when using `./build.sh`. For dark sites, you may download the plugins and place these same directory as your Packer executable `/usr/local/bin` or `$HOME/.packer.d/plugins`.
128	
129	  - [Packer Plugin for Git][packer-plugin-git] 0.6.2 or later - a community plugin for HashiCorp Packer.
130	  - HashiCorp [Packer Plugin for Proxmox-ISO][packer-plugin-proxmox] version 1.2.1 - the plugin for HashiCorp Packer to communicate with Proxmox VE. This needs to be pinned to version 1.2.1 at this time due to a [CPU bug](https://github.com/hashicorp/packer-plugin-proxmox/issues/307).
131	
132	**Ansible**:
133	
134	- [Ansible][ansible] [Core][ansible-core] version 2.14 or higher.
135	
136	  > **Note**
137	  >
138	  > Click on the operating system name to display the installation steps.
139	
140	  - <details>
141	      <summary>Ubuntu</summary>
142	
143	    It is recommended that you install ansible-core using your system's package manager instead of via pip.
144	
145	    Refresh the repositories:
146	    ```shell
147	    sudo apt update
148	    ```
149	
150	    Install software-properties-common:
151	    ```shell
152	    sudo apt install -y software-properties-common
153	    ```
154	
155	    Add the Ansible repository to your system:
156	
157	    ```shell
158	    sudo add-apt-repository --yes --update ppa:ansible/ansible
159	    ```
160	
161	    Install ansible-core from the Ansible repository:
162	
163	    ```shell
164	    sudo apt install -y ansible-core
165	    ```
166	
167	    </details>
168	
169	
170	  - <details>
171	      <summary>CentOS Stream 9</summary>
172	
173	    It is recommended that you install ansible using your system's package manager instead of via pip.
174	
175	    Install.
176	
177	    ```shell
178	    dnf -y install ansible
179	    ```
180	    </details>
181	
182	
183	**Platform**:
184	
185	- Proxmox PVE 8.0 or later.
186	
187	# Download
188	
189	After installing the required software, the quickest way to get building is to clone this repository.
190	
191	```shell
192	git clone https://github.com/traefikturkey/oncall.git
193	cd oncall
194	```
195	
196	The following table describes the directory structure.
197	
198	| Directory       | Description                                                                              |
199	| :---            | :---                                                                                     |
200	| **`ansible`**   | Contains the Ansible roles to prepare Linux machine image builds.                        |
201	| **`builds`**    | Contains the templates, variables, and configuration files for the machine image builds. |
202	| **`manifests`** | Manifests created after the completion of the machine image builds.                      |
203	
204	
205	# Configuration
206	
207	## Example Variables
208	
209	The project includes example variables files that you can use as a starting point for your own configuration.
210	
211	The [variables][packer-variables] are defined in `.pkrvars.hcl` files.
212	
213	Run the config script `./config.sh` to copy the `.pkrvars.hcl.example` files to a `config` directory.
214	
215	```shell
216	./config.sh
217	./build.sh
218	```
219	
220	The `config` folder is the default folder. You can override the default by passing an alternate value as the first argument.
221	
222	For example:
223	
224	San Francisco: `us-west-1`
225	
226	```shell
227	./config.sh us-west-1
228	./build.sh us-west-1
229	```
230	
231	Los Angeles: `us-west-2`
232	
233	```shell
234	./config.sh us-west-2
235	./build.sh us-west-2
236	```
237	
238	This is useful for the purposes of running machine image builds for different environment.
239	
240	## Configuration Variables
241	
242	## Proxmox Virtual Machine IDs
243	Each template has a preset static vm_id.
244	
245	These can be changed by editing the `./config/*/(linux or windows)-(*).pkr.hcl` files.
246	
247	### Ansible Variables
248	
249	Edit the `config/ansible.pkrvars.hcl` file to configure the credentials for the Ansible account on Linux machine images.
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 2026-05-07 16:16:31Z)**_

```sh
#!/usr/bin/env bash

# Docker Build Helper Script
# This script simplifies running builds using Docker

set -e

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"

# Colors for output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

print_header() {
    echo -e "${BLUE}"
    echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
    echo "║        Oncall - Docker Packer Build Helper                ║"
    echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
    echo -e "${NC}"
}

print_usage() {
    echo "Usage: $0 [OPTION]"
    echo ""
    echo "Options:"
    echo "  setup       - Initial setup: build image and create config"
    echo "  build       - Run interactive build menu"
    echo "  validate    - Validate all Packer templates"
    echo "  shell       - Open a shell in the container"
    echo "  clean       - Remove Docker images and containers"
    echo "  rebuild     - Rebuild Docker image from scratch"
    echo "  help        - Show this help message"
    echo ""
    echo "Examples:"
    echo "  $0 setup      # First-time setup"
    echo "  $0 build      # Run a build"
    echo "  $0 validate   # Validate all templates"
}

check_docker() {
    if ! command -v docker &> /dev/null; then
        echo -e "${RED}Error: Docker is not installed or not in PATH${NC}"
        echo "Please install Docker from: https://docs.docker.com/get-docker/"
        exit 1
    fi

    if ! command -v docker-compose &> /dev/null && ! docker compose version &> /dev/null; then
        echo -e "${RED}Error: Docker Compose is not installed${NC}"
        echo "Please install Docker Compose from: https://docs.docker.com/compose/install/"
        exit 1
    fi
}

check_config() {
    if [ ! -d "$SCRIPT_DIR/config" ] || [ -z "$(ls -A "$SCRIPT_DIR/config" 2>/dev/null)" ]; then
        echo -e "${YELLOW}Warning: Config directory is missing or empty${NC}"
        echo "Creating config files..."
        bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/config.sh"
        echo -e "${GREEN}Config files created in ./config/${NC}"
        echo -e "${YELLOW}Please edit the config files with your Proxmox settings before running builds${NC}"
        return 1
    fi
    return 0
}

build_image() {
    echo -e "${BLUE}Building Docker image...${NC}"
    docker-compose build
    echo -e "${GREEN}Docker image built successfully${NC}"
}

setup() {
    print_header
    echo -e "${BLUE}Running initial setup...${NC}"
    echo ""

    # Build Docker image
    build_image
    echo ""

    # Create config
    if [ ! -d "$SCRIPT_DIR/config" ]; then
        echo -e "${BLUE}Creating configuration files...${NC}"
        bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/config.sh"
        echo -e "${GREEN}Configuration files created in ./config/${NC}"
    else
        echo -e "${YELLOW}Config directory already exists, skipping...${NC}"
    fi

    echo ""
    echo -e "${GREEN}Setup complete!${NC}"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Next steps:${NC}"
    echo "  1. Edit configuration files in ./config/ with your Proxmox settings"
    echo "  2. Run: $0 build"
    echo ""
}

run_build() {
    print_header
    check_config || {
        echo ""
        echo "Please configure your settings in ./config/ and try again"
        exit 1
    }

    echo -e "${BLUE}Starting interactive build...${NC}"
    docker-compose run --rm packer ./build.sh "$@"
}

run_validate() {
    print_header
    check_config || {
        echo ""
        echo "Please configure your settings in ./config/ and try again"
        exit 1
    }

    echo -e "${BLUE}Validating Packer templates...${NC}"
    docker-compose run --rm packer ./validate.sh "$@"
}

run_shell() {
    print_header
    echo -e "${BLUE}Opening shell in container...${NC}"
    docker-compose run --rm packer /bin/bash
}

clean() {
    print_header
    echo -e "${YELLOW}This will remove Docker images and containers${NC}"
    echo -e "${RED}Are you sure? (y/n)${NC}"
    read -r response
    if [[ "$response" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
        echo -e "${BLUE}Cleaning up...${NC}"
        docker-compose down -v
        docker rmi oncall-packer:latest 2>/dev/null || true
        echo -e "${GREEN}Cleanup complete${NC}"
    else
        echo "Cancelled"
    fi
}

rebuild() {
    print_header
    echo -e "${BLUE}Rebuilding Docker image from scratch...${NC}"
    docker-compose build --no-cache
    echo -e "${GREEN}Rebuild complete${NC}"
}

# Main script
check_docker

case "${1:-}" in
    setup)
        setup
        ;;
    build)
        shift
        run_build "$@"
        ;;
    validate)
        shift
        run_validate "$@"
        ;;
    shell)
        run_shell
        ;;
    clean)
        clean
        ;;
    rebuild)
        rebuild
        ;;
    help|--help|-h)
        print_header
        print_usage
        ;;
    *)
        print_header
        echo -e "${RED}Error: Invalid option${NC}"
        echo ""
        print_usage
        exit 1
        ;;
esac

```

```md
# Quick Setup Guide

This guide will get you up and running with Oncall Packer builds in minutes.

## Prerequisites

- **Docker** and **Docker Compose** installed
- Access to a Proxmox server (v8.0+)
- Proxmox API token with appropriate permissions

## 3-Step Setup

### 1. Clone and Setup

\```bash
git clone https://github.com/traefikturkey/oncall.git
cd oncall
./docker-build.sh setup
\```

This will:
- Build the Docker image with all dependencies
- Create configuration template files in `./config/`

### 2. Configure

Edit the configuration files in the `config/` directory:

**Required files to edit:**

1. **`config/proxmox.pkrvars.hcl`** - Proxmox connection settings
   \```hcl
   proxmox_api_token_id        = "your-user@pam!your-token"
   proxmox_api_token_secret    = "your-secret"
   proxmox_hostname            = "proxmox.example.com"
   proxmox_node                = "your-node-name"
   \```

2. **`config/build.pkrvars.hcl`** - Build user credentials
   \```hcl
   build_username           = "yourusername"
   build_password           = "yourpassword"
   build_password_encrypted = "sha512-hash"  # Generate with: mkpasswd -m sha512crypt
   build_key                = "your-ssh-public-key"
   \```

3. **`config/ansible.pkrvars.hcl`** - Ansible user credentials
   \```hcl
   ansible_username = "ansible"
   ansible_key      = "ansible-ssh-public-key"
   \```

4. **`config/network.pkrvars.hcl`** - Network settings
   \```hcl
   vm_bridge_interface = "vmbr0"
   vm_vlan_tag         = "102"
   # Optional: Set static IP (comment out for DHCP)
   # vm_ip_address = "192.168.1.100"
   # vm_ip_netmask = 24
   # vm_ip_gateway = "192.168.1.1"
   # vm_dns_list   = ["8.8.8.8", "8.8.4.4"]
   \```

5. **`config/common.pkrvars.hcl`** - Common settings
   \```hcl
   common_iso_storage = "local"  # Your ISO storage location
   \```

6. **`config/linux-storage.pkrvars.hcl`** - Storage configuration
   \```hcl
   vm_efi_storage_pool = "local-lvm"
   vm_storage_pool     = "local-lvm"
   vm_disk_device      = "vda"
   # Configure partitions as needed
   \```

### 3. Build

Run a build:

\```bash
./docker-build.sh build
\```

Select your desired OS from the menu.

## Available Commands

\```bash
./docker-build.sh setup      # Initial setup
./docker-build.sh build      # Run interactive build
./docker-build.sh validate   # Validate all templates
./docker-build.sh shell      # Open shell in container
./docker-build.sh clean      # Remove Docker resources
./docker-build.sh rebuild    # Rebuild image from scratch
\```

## Troubleshooting

### Config Issues

If you get configuration errors:

1. Ensure all required files in `config/` are edited
2. Check Proxmox credentials are correct
3. Verify network connectivity to Proxmox server

### Build Issues

- **Timeout errors**: Increase timeout in the OS-specific `.pkrvars.hcl` file
- **ISO not found**: Check `common_iso_storage` and ensure ISO is uploaded to Proxmox
- **Network issues**: Verify firewall allows ports 8000-8099 for HTTP server

### Validation

Before building, validate templates:

\```bash
./docker-build.sh validate
\```

## What Gets Built

Templates are created in Proxmox with:
- Pre-installed and configured OS
- QEMU guest agent
- Cloud-init support (if enabled)
- SSH access configured
- Ansible user configured
- All specified packages installed

## File Locations

- **Configurations**: `./config/`
- **Build manifests**: `./manifests/`
- **Packer templates**: `./builds/`
- **Ansible roles**: `./ansible/`

## Next Steps

After your first successful build:

1. Test the template by cloning it in Proxmox
2. Verify cloud-init configuration
3. Create additional builds for other OS versions
4. Customize storage layouts in `config/linux-storage.pkrvars.hcl`
5. Add custom packages in OS-specific `.pkrvars.hcl` files

## Additional Resources

- [DOCKER.md](DOCKER.md) - Detailed Docker usage
- [README.md](README.md) - Full project documentation
- [Proxmox API Tokens](https://pve.proxmox.com/pve-docs/pveum-plain.html) - How to create API tokens

## Support

- GitHub Issues: https://github.com/traefikturkey/oncall/issues
- Original Project: https://github.com/ajschroeder/proxmox-packer-examples

```

# Changes Summary

This document summarizes the changes made to add Docker containerization and Ubuntu 26.04 support.

## Date: 2026-05-07

## Changes Made

### 1. Docker Containerization

Added full Docker support to make the project portable and easier to use across different platforms.

#### New Files Created:

- **`Dockerfile`** - Production-ready container image with:
  - Ubuntu 24.04 base
  - Packer 1.12.0
  - Ansible Core 2.17
  - All required dependencies pre-installed
  - Support for mounted config directories

- **`docker-compose.yml`** - Docker Compose configuration with:
  - Main service for interactive use
  - Volume mounts for config, manifests, and SSH keys
  - Host networking mode for Proxmox connectivity
  - Build service profile for CI/CD

- **`.dockerignore`** - Optimized Docker build context

- **`docker-build.sh`** - Helper script with commands:
  - `setup` - Initial setup and image build
  - `build` - Run interactive build menu
  - `validate` - Validate all templates
  - `shell` - Open container shell
  - `clean` - Remove Docker resources
  - `rebuild` - Rebuild image from scratch

#### Documentation Added:

- **`DOCKER.md`** - Comprehensive Docker usage guide including:
  - Quick start instructions
  - Configuration management
  - Advanced usage patterns
  - Troubleshooting tips
  - CI/CD integration examples

- **`SETUP.md`** - Quick setup guide for new users:
  - 3-step setup process
  - Configuration file explanations
  - Troubleshooting section
  - Next steps guidance

#### README Updates:

- Added "Docker Setup (Recommended)" section
- Updated Table of Contents
- Added Docker quick start instructions
- Highlighted benefits of Docker setup

### 2. Ubuntu 26.04 LTS Support

Merged the `feature/add-ubuntu-26-04` branch which includes:

#### Files Added/Modified:

- **`builds/linux/ubuntu/26-04-lts/`** - Complete Ubuntu 26.04 build configuration:
  - `linux-ubuntu.pkr.hcl` - Main Packer template
  - `variables.pkr.hcl` - Variable definitions
  - `variables-network.pkr.hcl` - Network variables
  - `variables-storage.pkr.hcl` - Storage variables
  - `linux-ubuntu.pkrvars.hcl.example` - Example configuration
  - `data/user-data.pkrtpl.hcl` - Cloud-init user data template
  - `data/network.pkrtpl.hcl` - Network configuration template
  - `data/storage.pkrtpl.hcl` - Storage configuration template
  - `data/meta-data` - Cloud-init metadata

- **`build.sh`** - Added menu option 21 for Ubuntu 26.04
- **`validate.sh`** - Added Ubuntu 26.04 to validation list
- **`README.md`** - Added Ubuntu 26.04 to OS support table (vm_id: 10015)
- **`ansible/roles/configure/tasks/ubuntu.yml`** - Updated for Ubuntu 26.04 support
- **`.gitignore`** - Updated with submodule ignores

#### Test Suite Added:

- **`tests/ubuntu-qemu/`** - QEMU-based smoke tests for Ubuntu:
  - Quick validation without full Proxmox setup
  - Tests cloud-image functionality
  - Validates SSH key configuration

### 3. Features and Benefits

#### Docker Benefits:
- ✅ **Portability**: Works on Windows, macOS, and Linux
- ✅ **No Manual Setup**: All dependencies pre-installed
- ✅ **Consistency**: Same environment for all users
- ✅ **Isolation**: Doesn't affect host system
- ✅ **CI/CD Ready**: Easy integration with pipelines

#### Ubuntu 26.04 Features:
- ✅ Full support for UEFI and BIOS boot
- ✅ Custom storage configuration
- ✅ Static IP and DHCP support
- ✅ Cloud-init enabled
- ✅ Fixed SSH key support
- ✅ Quiet MOTD during configuration

## File Structure Changes
