Excellent idea! Let me create a comprehensive PowerShell script that handles all the Docker setup for Windows users:

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 2026-05-08 16:20:19Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```ps1
#!/usr/bin/env pwsh
<#
.SYNOPSIS
    Launch Packer container with proper Windows networking configuration
.DESCRIPTION
    This script handles the quirks of running Packer in Docker Desktop on Windows:
    - Verifies GitHub authentication
    - Detects the correct host IP for Proxmox connectivity
    - Configures HTTP bind address for Packer
    - Launches container with appropriate port mappings
.PARAMETER Shell
    Launch interactive shell (default)
.PARAMETER Build
    Launch build.sh directly
.PARAMETER Validate
    Launch validate.sh directly
.EXAMPLE
    .\docker-run.ps1
    .\docker-run.ps1 -Build
    .\docker-run.ps1 -Validate
#>

[CmdletBinding()]
param(
    [Parameter()]
    [switch]$Shell,

    [Parameter()]
    [switch]$Build,

    [Parameter()]
    [switch]$Validate
)

# Set strict mode
Set-StrictMode -Version Latest
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'

# Banner
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "=====================================================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "  MTEA FSG - Docker Container Launcher (Windows)" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "=====================================================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host ""

# Step 1: Check GitHub CLI authentication
Write-Host "[1/5] Checking GitHub CLI authentication..." -ForegroundColor Yellow

try {
    $ghStatus = gh auth status 2>&1 | Out-String

    if ($ghStatus -match "Logged in to github.com") {
        Write-Host "  ✓ GitHub CLI authenticated" -ForegroundColor Green

        # Check for read:packages scope
        if ($ghStatus -match "read:packages" -or $ghStatus -match "repo") {
            Write-Host "  ✓ Required scopes present" -ForegroundColor Green
        } else {
            Write-Host "  ⚠ Warning: Missing 'read:packages' scope" -ForegroundColor Yellow
            Write-Host "  Run: gh auth refresh -s read:packages" -ForegroundColor Yellow
        }
    } else {
        Write-Host "  ✗ Not logged in to GitHub" -ForegroundColor Red
        Write-Host ""
        Write-Host "Please authenticate with GitHub CLI first:" -ForegroundColor Yellow
        Write-Host "  gh auth login" -ForegroundColor Cyan
        Write-Host "  gh auth refresh -s read:packages" -ForegroundColor Cyan
        exit 1
    }
} catch {
    Write-Host "  ✗ GitHub CLI not found or not working" -ForegroundColor Red
    Write-Host ""
    Write-Host "Please install GitHub CLI:" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    Write-Host "  winget install --id GitHub.cli" -ForegroundColor Cyan
    exit 1
}

Write-Host ""

# Step 2: Detect Windows IP for Proxmox connectivity
Write-Host "[2/5] Detecting Windows host IP for Proxmox connectivity..." -ForegroundColor Yellow

$ipAddresses = Get-NetIPAddress -AddressFamily IPv4 |
    Where-Object {
        $_.IPAddress -notlike "127.*" -and
        $_.IPAddress -notlike "169.254.*" -and
        $_.IPAddress -notlike "192.168.65.*" -and  # Docker Desktop internal
        $_.PrefixOrigin -ne "WellKnown"
    } |
    Select-Object IPAddress, InterfaceAlias, PrefixLength

if ($ipAddresses.Count -eq 0) {
    Write-Host "  ✗ No suitable network interface found" -ForegroundColor Red
    Write-Host ""
    Write-Host "Please ensure you have a network connection to Proxmox" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    exit 1
} elseif ($ipAddresses.Count -eq 1) {
    $selectedIP = $ipAddresses[0].IPAddress
    $selectedInterface = $ipAddresses[0].InterfaceAlias
    Write-Host "  ✓ Detected IP: $selectedIP ($selectedInterface)" -ForegroundColor Green
} else {
    Write-Host "  Multiple network interfaces detected:" -ForegroundColor Cyan
    Write-Host ""
    for ($i = 0; $i -lt $ipAddresses.Count; $i++) {
        $ip = $ipAddresses[$i]
        Write-Host "    [$($i+1)] $($ip.IPAddress) - $($ip.InterfaceAlias)" -ForegroundColor White
    }
    Write-Host ""

    $selection = Read-Host "  Select interface number [1-$($ipAddresses.Count)]"
    $index = [int]$selection - 1

    if ($index -lt 0 -or $index -ge $ipAddresses.Count) {
        Write-Host "  ✗ Invalid selection" -ForegroundColor Red
        exit 1
    }

    $selectedIP = $ipAddresses[$index].IPAddress
    $selectedInterface = $ipAddresses[$index].InterfaceAlias
    Write-Host "  ✓ Using: $selectedIP ($selectedInterface)" -ForegroundColor Green
}

Write-Host ""

# Step 3: Check Docker
Write-Host "[3/5] Checking Docker..." -ForegroundColor Yellow

try {
    $dockerVersion = docker version --format '{{.Server.Version}}' 2>&1
    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
        Write-Host "  ✓ Docker is running (v$dockerVersion)" -ForegroundColor Green
    } else {
        throw "Docker not running"
    }
} catch {
    Write-Host "  ✗ Docker is not running" -ForegroundColor Red
    Write-Host ""
    Write-Host "Please start Docker Desktop and try again" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    exit 1
}

Write-Host ""

# Step 4: Pull/check image
Write-Host "[4/5] Checking Docker image..." -ForegroundColor Yellow

$imageName = "ghcr.io/eagletg-development/dev-packer:latest"

$imageExists = docker images --format "{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}" | Select-String -Pattern "^$([regex]::Escape($imageName))$" -Quiet

if (-not $imageExists) {
    Write-Host "  Image not found locally. Pulling from GitHub Container Registry..." -ForegroundColor Yellow

    # Login to ghcr.io
    try {
        $ghToken = gh auth token
        $ghToken | docker login ghcr.io -u $env:USERNAME --password-stdin 2>&1 | Out-Null
    } catch {
        Write-Host "  ⚠ Could not login to ghcr.io automatically" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    }

    docker pull $imageName

    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
        Write-Host "  ✗ Failed to pull image" -ForegroundColor Red
        Write-Host ""
        Write-Host "Try manually:" -ForegroundColor Yellow
        Write-Host "  gh auth token | docker login ghcr.io -u YOUR_USERNAME --password-stdin" -ForegroundColor Cyan
        Write-Host "  docker pull $imageName" -ForegroundColor Cyan
        exit 1
    }
}

Write-Host "  ✓ Image ready: $imageName" -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host ""

# Step 5: Launch container
Write-Host "[5/5] Launching container..." -ForegroundColor Yellow

$httpPorts = "8000-8099"
$packerHttpAddr = "${selectedIP}:8000"

Write-Host "  HTTP Bind Address: $packerHttpAddr" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "  Port Mapping: $httpPorts -> $httpPorts" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host ""

# Determine command to run
$containerCommand = "/bin/bash"
if ($Build) {
    $containerCommand = "./build.sh"
    Write-Host "  Mode: Build" -ForegroundColor Cyan
} elseif ($Validate) {
    $containerCommand = "./validate.sh"
    Write-Host "  Mode: Validate" -ForegroundColor Cyan
} else {
    Write-Host "  Mode: Interactive Shell" -ForegroundColor Cyan
}

Write-Host ""
Write-Host "=====================================================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host ""

# Build docker run command
$dockerArgs = @(
    "run"
    "-it"
    "--rm"
    "-p", "${httpPorts}:${httpPorts}"
    "-e", "PACKER_HTTP_ADDR=$packerHttpAddr"
    "-v", "${PWD}:/workspace"
    "-v", "${PWD}/config:/workspace/config"
    "-v", "${PWD}/manifests:/workspace/manifests"
    $imageName
)

if ($containerCommand -ne "/bin/bash") {
    $dockerArgs += $containerCommand
}

# Execute
& docker $dockerArgs

```

596	
597	### Docker Benefits
598	
599	- ✅ **Consistent Environment** - Same tools/versions across all systems
600	- ✅ **Portability** - Works on Windows, macOS, Linux without manual setup
601	- ✅ **Isolation** - No pollution of host system
602	- ✅ **Easy Updates** - Pull latest image or rebuild locally
603	- ✅ **Team Collaboration** - Everyone uses identical environment
604	- ✅ **Pre-Built Images** - Available from GitHub Container Registry (ghcr.io)
605	- ✅ **CI/CD Ready** - Automated builds on every commit
606	
607	---
608	
609	## Advanced Configuration
610	
611	### Per-OS Template Settings
612	
613	Each OS has a config file: `config/linux-<os>-<version>.pkrvars.hcl`
614	
615	Example `config/linux-ubuntu-24-04-lts.pkrvars.hcl`:
616	
617	```hcl
618	// Guest OS Settings
619	vm_os_language = "en_US"
620	vm_os_keyboard = "us"
621	vm_os_timezone = "America/Los_Angeles"
622	
623	// Hardware
624	vm_cpu_count   = 2
625	vm_cpu_sockets = 1
626	vm_cpu_type    = "host"  // "host" for best performance
627	vm_mem_size    = 4096
628	vm_disk_size   = "40G"
629
```

```text
667	## Troubleshooting
668	
669	### Configuration Issues
670	
671	**Problem**: Build fails with config errors
672	
673	**Solution**:
674	1. Verify all required config files edited
675	2. Check Proxmox credentials are correct
676	3. Test connectivity: `ping proxmox.mtea.local`
677	
678	### Build Timeouts
679	
680	**Problem**: Build exceeds timeout
681	
682	**Solution**: Increase timeout in OS-specific variables:
683	
684	```hcl
685	// In config/linux-ubuntu-24-04-lts.pkrvars.hcl
686	variable "timeout" {
687	  default = "120m"  // Increase from default 90m
688	}
689	```
690	
691	### ISO Not Found
692	
693	**Problem**: Packer can't find ISO file
694	
695	**Solution**:
696	1. Verify ISO uploaded to Proxmox storage
697	2. Check `common_iso_storage` matches storage name
698	3. Confirm `iso_path` and `iso_file` are correct
699	4. Test: Navigate to Proxmox web UI → Storage → ISO images
700	
701	### Network Connectivity
702	
703	**Problem**: Can't reach Proxmox or HTTP server timeouts
704	
705	**Solution**:
706	1. Verify `vm_bridge_interface` exists: `pvesh get /nodes/<node>/network`
707	2. Check VLAN tag matches network config
708	3. Ensure firewall allows ports 8000-8099
709	4. Test: `curl http://<packer-host>:8000/`
710	5. For Docker: Verify `network_mode: host` in docker-compose.yml
711	
712	### SSH Connection Failures
713	
714	**Problem**: Packer can't SSH to VM
715	
716	**Solution**:
```

 `https://www.virtualizationhowto.com/2026/04/i-use-packer-to-automate-ubuntu-server-26-04-on-proxmox-and-it-saves-me-hours/`

```text
# Packer Configuration for Ubuntu 26.04 on Proxmox - Technical Details

## Cloud-Init Configuration & Timeout Handling

The template implements cloud-init integration with specific cleanup provisioning. After the autoinstall completes, Packer executes shell commands that:

- Wait for cloud-init to finish: `"while [ ! -f /var/lib/cloud/instance/boot-finished ]; do echo 'Waiting for cloud-init...'; sleep 1; done"`
- Remove SSH host keys and machine IDs to ensure uniqueness in clones
- Clean package caches and cloud-init state
- Set SSH timeout to `"20m"` to accommodate longer installations

The configuration also removes `"subiquity-disable-cloudinit-networking.cfg"` and installer netplan files to allow cloud-init full network control.

## Network Setup & HTTP Server Configuration

HTTP server binding is handled through variables:

```
http_bind_address = "${var.http_ip}"
```

The default HTTP IP is `"10.1.149.27"`. This setup becomes necessary when Packer machines have multiple network interfaces. Optional port binding ranges can be specified via `http_port_min` and `http_port_max` parameters.

## Boot Commands & Autoinstall Settings

The boot sequence uses UEFI interrupt to inject autoinstall parameters:

```
"autoinstall ds=nocloud-net\\;s=http://{{ .HTTPIP }}:{{ .HTTPPort }}/ ---"
```

Boot waits 5 seconds before executing these commands, allowing the installer to become responsive.

## Variable Configurations

Three essential variables are required:
- `proxmox_api_url`: API endpoint (e.g., `"https://pvehost01.cloud.local:8006/api2/json"`)
- `proxmox_api_token_id`: Authentication credential
- `proxmox_api_token_secret`: Sensitive token marked for masking

The documentation recommends storing sensitive values in a separate `variables.tfvars` file excluded from version control.

## User-Data & Meta-Data Examples

The meta-data file remains blank. User-data specifies:
- Locale and keyboard layout (US)
- SSH server installation with password authentication enabled
- Package additions including `"qemu-guest-agent"` for Proxmox integration
- Direct storage layout with zero swap
- Single user "ubuntu" with passwordless sudo access via group membership

No special Ubuntu 26.04 workarounds are documented in the provided configuration.
```

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 2026-05-08 16:27:06Z)**_

Perfect! Now let's also check if the article mentions anything about the HTTP bind address configuration:

The article mentions they use:
```hcl
http_bind_address = "${var.http_ip}"
```

With default `http_ip = "10.1.149.27"`. This is **exactly what `docker-run.ps1` handles automatically** for Windows users.

## Summary of Fixes Applied

**From the article:**
1. ✅ **Added shell provisioner** to wait for cloud-init completion before Ansible runs
   - Prevents Packer from trying to connect while cloud-init is still running
   - Applied to Ubuntu 20.04, 22.04, 24.04, and 26.04

**Already solved by `docker-run.ps1`:**
2. ✅ **HTTP bind address configuration** - Your new script automatically detects the correct Windows IP and sets `PACKER_HTTP_ADDR`

**What to do now:**

1. **Test the fix** - Use the `docker-run.ps1` script to launch the container:
   ```powershell
   .\docker-run.ps1 -Build
   ```

2. The script will:
   - Detect your Windows IP that Proxmox can reach
   - Set `PACKER_HTTP_ADDR` environment variable
   - Launch container with proper port mapping

3. Inside the container, run `./build.sh` and select Ubuntu 26.04

The cloud-init timeout should be completely resolved now because Packer will **wait** for cloud-init to finish before attempting SSH/Ansible provisioning.

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 2026-05-08 16:28:01Z)**_

Perfect! Now you can run with debug logging:

```powershell
# Run with debug logging enabled
.\docker-run.ps1 -Build -Debug
```

**What this does:**
- Sets `PACKER_LOG=1` (enables verbose debug output)
- Sets `PACKER_LOG_PATH=/workspace/packer-debug.log` (writes to file)
- The log file will be created in your current directory as `packer-debug.log`

**After the build (success or failure):**
```powershell
# View the debug log
cat packer-debug.log | more

# Search for specific issues
Select-String -Path packer-debug.log -Pattern "error|timeout|fail" -Context 2,2

# Search for HTTP server issues
Select-String -Path packer-debug.log -Pattern "HTTP|HTTPIP|192.168.65" -Context 2,2
```

The debug log will show you exactly:
- What IP Packer is using for the HTTP server
- Whether the VM can reach it
- Cloud-init progress
- SSH connection attempts
- Everything Packer is doing step-by-step

This will make troubleshooting much easier!

Now the debug output will go to the console. When you set `PACKER_LOG=1` without `PACKER_LOG_PATH`, it outputs to stderr/stdout.

Try it now:

```powershell
# Debug output to console
.\docker-run.ps1 -Build -PackerDebug

# Or capture debug output to file
.\docker-run.ps1 -Build -PackerDebug | Tee-Object packer-debug.log

# Just open a shell with debug enabled (can run builds manually inside)
.\docker-run.ps1 -PackerDebug
```

The `-PackerDebug` flag works independently - it just sets the `PACKER_LOG=1` environment variable in the container, which will be active for any Packer command you run (whether via `-Build` or manually inside the shell).

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 2026-05-08 16:48:25Z)**_

Perfect! Now the setup is complete:

1. **docker-run.ps1** detects your physical Ethernet IP (10.0.43.25)
2. Sets `PACKER_HTTP_ADDR=10.0.43.25:8000` environment variable
3. **build.sh** reads that variable and passes it to Packer as `-var common_http_bind_address=10.0.43.25:8000`
4. Packer binds its HTTP server to your Windows IP instead of the Docker internal IP
5. The Proxmox VM can reach the HTTP server during autoinstall

**Test it now:**

```powershell
.\docker-run.ps1 -PackerDebug
```

You should see:
```
Using PACKER_HTTP_ADDR: 10.0.43.25:8000
```

Then select Ubuntu 26.04 and the build should work! The VM will be able to reach the HTTP server at your Windows IP, and cloud-init won't hang waiting for unreachable resources.
